description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn attached to a curved arm, which is mounted on a polished wooden base. The base is rectangular with beveled edges and houses a visible turntable platter, which holds a black vinyl record with a brown label at its center. A metallic crank handle protrudes from the side of the wooden box, suggesting manual operation. The tonearm is cream-colored with a metallic joint, positioned over the record, and appears to be fixed in place. The horn is textured with visible ridges and a matte finish, giving it an antique appearance. No modern control knobs, switches, or dust cover are visible, emphasizing its classic, mechanical design.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, and visible vinyl record. Features a crank handle and classic tonearm, with no modern controls or dust cover.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing the large brass horn, wooden base, crank handle, and the vinyl record on the platter.
description_view_1: Direct view into the horn, highlighting its flared shape and inner texture, with the base and record partially obscured.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the full curve of the tonearm, the horn's attachment, and the record platter on the wooden base.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back of the horn, the crank handle, and the record platter, with the tonearm's mounting more visible.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and circular, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It is black, likely made of metal or hard plastic, and holds a vinyl record with a brown label. The platter appears smooth with a slight sheen and is proportionally smaller than the horn but large enough to accommodate a standard record. No visible grooves or intricate patterns are present on the platter itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, cream-colored, and gently curved, extending from a mounting point on the right side of the base toward the center of the platter. It appears to be made of metal or painted wood, with a metallic joint at the base. The tonearm is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready for playback.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its location is implied at the end of the tonearm, where it would typically contact the record.
